titleStmt.title: SII 26.37: original edition by P. R. Srinivasan
No. 37. (A.R. No. 36 of 1909.) KUṈṈAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.

summary.p: On the second and third pillars in the same place.
The record commences with Pūmaruviya tirumaḍandaiyum, the praśasti of the king who is described as Śōṉāḍu-vaḻaṅgiyarḷuliya.

It records the gift of paddy perhaps purchased out of the money got from the produce of lands in Niyamam, one of the villages irrigated by (the river) Tēnāṟu in Kēraḻasiṅga-vaḷanāḍu to a deity (name lost) probably by Gaṇḍaṉ Āḻuḍaiyāṉ alias Kalvāyil-nāḍāḻvāṉ.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . pūmaruviya tirumaṭantaiyum puvimaṭantaiyum pu
yattiruppa ṉāmaruviya kalaimaṭantaiyum ceyamaṭantaiyum nalañ
ciṟappak koḷārnta ciṉappuliyuṅ koṭuñcilaiyuṅ kulainto
ḷippa vāḷārnta poṟkirimel varikkayalkaḷ viḷaiyāṭa Iruṅkaṭal va
layattiṉitaṟam perukak karuṅkalikaṭintu ceṅkolṉaṭappa Oru
kuṭai ṉiḻaliliruṉilaṅ kuḷira muvvakait tamiḻa muṟaimaiyil viḷaṅka ṉā
lvakai vetamum naviṉṟuṭaṉ vaḷara Aivakai veḻviyuñ ceyviṉai
yiyaṟṟa Aṟuvakaic camaiyamumaḻakuṭaṉ tikaḻa Eḻuvakaipāṭalum Iyal
puṭaṉ parava Eṇṭicaiyaḷavuñ cakkarañcella koṅkaṇar kaliṅkar koca
lar māḷavar ciṅkaḷar teviṅkar cinar kuccarar villavar makatar vikkalar
cempiyar pallavar mutaliyar pāttivarellā muṟaiviṭa maruḷeṉa Oruvar
muṉṉoruvar muṟaimuṟai kaṭava tantiṟaikuṇarntiṟaiñca Ilaṅko
ḷa maṇimuṭi Intiraṉ pūṭṭiya polaṅkatirāram mārviṉiṟ poliyappaṉimala
rttāmaraitticaimukaṉ paṭaitta maṉuneṟi taḻaippa maṇimuṭi cūṭippoṉṉi
cūḻnāṭṭiṟ puliyāṇai poyakalak kaṉṉicūḻnāṭṭu kayalāḷai
kai vaḷara veñcilai vekuḷiyum veḻamum parappit tañcaiyumuṟantai
yuñ centaḻal koḷuttiThe words kāviyum nilamum, niṉṟu kaviṉamum are omitted here.Āviyu māṟum Aṇinirnilaṉaḻittuk kūṭa
mum māmatiḷuṅ kopuramum māṭamum māḷikaiyum maṇṭapamum pala
viṭittut toḻutu vantaṭaiyā nirupartan tokaiyar Amutakaṇ
ṇirāṟu parappik kaḻutaikoṇṭuḻutu kavaṭi vitti cempiyaṉaic ciṉa
miriyapporutu curam pukayoṭṭippaiym poṉmaṇi muṭipaṟit
tup pāṇaṉukkuk kuṭuttaruḷi pāṭaluñ ciṟappiṟ paruti vāṉṟoy Ā
ṭakapparucai Āyirattaḷiyilc coravaḷavaṉ Api(ḻ)cekamaṇṭapa
ttu virāabhiṣekañ ceytu pukaḷvirittu nāḷum palarācaṉ
nāmattalai piṭuṅki miḷuntaṟukaṇmatayāṉai melkoṇṭu
nirāḻivaiyam muḻutum potuvoḻippak kūrāḻiyuñ ceyya to
ḷuḷtuṇaiyāka Aiyappaṭāta Arumaṟaicerantaṇar vāḻ
teyvappuliyūr tiruvellaiyuṭpukkup poṉṉampalam po
viya Āṭuvār pūvaiyuṭaṉ maṇṇun tirumeṉi kaṇṭu maṉaṅkaḷi
ppak kolamalar melayaṉuṅ kuḷirtuḻāy mālumaṟiyarmalar
cevaṭi vaṇaṅki ṭaiyāṉai ma liṭai co
vāṅku ciṟaiyaṉṉantukilloḻiya vaṇṭeḻuppum pūṅkamalavāḻicūḻ poṉ
ṉamarāpatiyil Ottulakantāṅkimuyar mevi Āruvai kuṇantu vaittaṉaiya co
timaṇimaṇṭapattiruntu colaimalipaḻaṉac coṇāṭu tāṉiḻantuThe passage from mālaimuṭiyuntara to puṟattu is omitted here.poṉvaḷa(ḷa)var Urimai
yoṭum pukuntu peṟṟa putalvaṉai niṉpereṉṟu muṉkāṭṭi veṟṟiyariyaṇaikkiḻ viḻntu
toḻutirappa tāṉoṭimuṉṉiḻanta vemmaiyellāṅkeyakala tāṉotakampaṇṇit
taṇṭār muṭiyuṭaṉe viṭṭa pukaliṭantaṉ mārveḷaikkuttāyam Iṭṭaṭikkeṉṟitu pi
ṭipāṭā �ṉkappoṅkutirai ñālattup pūpālar toḷ viḷaṅkac ceṅkayal
koṇṭuṉṟun tirumukamum paṇṭiḻanta coḻapatimeṉṉu nāmamuñ toṉṉakarumi
ḷa vaḻaṅkiviṭaikuṭuttuviṭṭaruḷivākaikkatirvel vaṭaveṉtar tam pātam meka
ttaṉai paṇiya virakkaḻalaṇintu viḷaṅkiya maṇimuṭi viraciṅṅācaṉattu vaḷaṅkeḻu ka
vari yirumaruṅkacaippa kaṭaleṉa muḻaṅkuṅkāṭṭunalliyāṉai vaṭapalaventar maṇip
puyam piriyā Ilaṅkukuḻaiyarivaiyar toḻutu niṉṟetta Ulakam muḻutuṭaiyāḷuṭaṉ vi
ṟṟiruntaruḷiya komutaṟ komāṟapaṉmarāṉa tiripuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ coṇāṭuvaḻaṅki
yaruḷiya śrīcuntarapāṇṭiyatevaṟku yāṇṭu munṟāvatin Etirāmāṇṭu keraḷaciṅkavaḷa
ṉāṭṭut teṉāṟṟuppokku niyamattukkuṉ nañcey puṉcey Uṭkiṭai
ṉṟi A ṉāṉa lukku Icaintu vilai
nellu Ippiḷḷaiyārkku tiru Amutu paṭikkum
Uṭalāka Aḷappatākavum Ippaṭicantirātittavaṟ ce
lvatāka nirumvāṟtu muttum Ippaṭi
ppaṭikkallilum veṭṭikkoḷka Ippaṭi kaṇṭaṉ Āḷu
ṭaiyāṉ Āṉa kalvāyilnāṭāḻvān Eḻuttu
Aṟivāparaṇa muventaveḷā
n Eḻuttu svastiśrī ḥ§
panmāheśvararakṣai .
Digital edition of SII 26.37 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
